description_long: This is a traditional European-style windmill with a stone masonry tower and a steeply pitched, shingled roof. Four large wooden blades, each with a lattice framework and fabric sails, are mounted on a central axis at the front of the structure. The stonework is weathered, with visible mortar lines and a slightly irregular base, suggesting age and durability. The roof is dark and textured, likely made of slate or similar material, and overhangs the tower slightly. The windmill's blades are the most prominent feature, extending well beyond the width of the tower and designed to catch the wind efficiently. The overall appearance is rustic and historic, suitable for placement in parks, rural neighborhoods, or as a landmark in an urban setting.
description: A historic stone windmill with a shingled roof and four large wooden blades with fabric sails, exuding a rustic, old-world charm.
description_view_0: Front view showing all four windmill blades and the full face of the stone tower.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the stone tower and the overhanging shingled roof, with blades seen edge-on.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the stone tower and the roof, with the windmill blades extending outward.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the stonework and roof profile, with the windmill blades again seen edge-on.
